name: IIOT-OXYS, Inc.
business: IIOT-OXYS designs, builds, tests, and sells edge computing systems for the Industrial Internet of Things, focusing on monitoring and predictive maintenance for industrial equipment and infrastructure. The company develops hardware, software, and algorithms that collect and analyze data locally to provide insights for machine uptime, energy efficiency, and quality improvement.
sellsTo: Medical Devices, Biotech, and Pharmaceutical Supply Chain

exposures.sentence: We specialize in edge computing, where the data processing is done locally right where the data is collected.
We help our customers maintain machine uptime and maximize operational efficiency.
We develop hardware, software and algorithms that monitor, measure and predict conditions for energy, structural, agricultural and medical applications.
We develop hardware, software and algorithms that monitor, measure and predict conditions for energy, structural, agricultural and medical applications.
We use off-the-shelf components, with reconfigurable hardware architecture that adapts to a wide range of customer needs and applications.

For bridges and other civil infrastructure, local, state and federal agencies have limited resources. We help our clients prioritize how to spend limited funds by addressing those fixes which need to be made first.

tamClaims.claim: The global smart manufacturing market was $233.3 billion in 2024 and will reach $479 billion by 2029 (CAGR 15.5%).
tamClaims.sentence: The global smart manufacturing (also known as Industry 4.0) was 233.3 billion in 2024 and will reach $479 billion by 2029 (CAGR 15.5%)

tamClaims.claim: The worldwide structural health monitoring industry is $2.5 billion in 2024 and will reach $4.1 billion by 2029 (CAGR of 10.4%).
tamClaims.sentence: the worldwide SHM industry is $2.5 billion in 2024 and will reach $4.1 billion by 2029 (CAGR of 10.4%)
